title: rubus anglicus
intro: Rubus anglicus, also known as the English bramble, is a type of plant that belongs to the rose family and is characterized by its thorny stems and white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus anglicus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus anglicus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
